pain_points: Rigid compliance requirements (CMMC, FedRAMP, NIST SP 800-171/800-53)
Security boundary verification for LLM agent actions
Fear of unauthorized API execution or data egress from secure enclaves
customer_segments.authority_required: Authority to Operate (ATO) for AI components

pain_points: HIPAA or SOC 2 compliance for LLM-driven workflow automation
Deterministic audit trails proving that agents did not perform unauthorized transactions
Model drift leading to security policy violations
Enterprise procurement security reviews blocking sales
Lack of standardized documentation to prove agent safety to enterprise CISOs
Insecure direct tool execution by customer-facing agents

demographics.deployment_state: Deploying LLM agents in production or late-stage staging

buyer_personas.focus: Risk mitigation, regulatory compliance, prevention of unauthorized egress/actions
buyer_personas.hook: We map your AI agents to NIST 800-53 controls so you can audit them like traditional software.

buyer_personas.role: VP of Engineering / Head of AI
buyer_personas.focus: Development velocity, enterprise readiness, security compliance without slowing down feature releases
buyer_personas.hook: Unblock enterprise sales by providing standard trust maps and privilege boundaries.

buyer_personas.focus: Documentation, control logging, evidence collection for SOC 2 or RMF audits
buyer_personas.hook: Generate automated evidence packages of agent executions for your auditor.
